rationale: Both miss the precommit-fix and benchmark-run asks entirely, but the human reviewers' one substantive concern was whether 'first prompt is parent' is guaranteed — Y directly and rigorously resolves this by tracing orchestrator.py's construction of [output, *companions] and confirming no caller path breaks on the new dict|None return, closely mirroring the actual resolution given in the PR thread. X notes the same point but more superficially in a validation bullet. X compensates with more numbered, highly actionable nits (concrete line/patch suggestions), though several concern pre-existing code not introduced by this diff, slightly denting precision. Both avoid fabrication and no gap-check section exists in either.
